Source_Species.reason: The ACO genes discussed are switchgrass genes from transcriptome/qRT-PCR analyses in this study.

Applied_Species.reason: ACO expression and ethylene measurements were conducted in switchgrass.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: ACO genes were analyzed under 300 mM NaCl; exogenous ET/ACC treatments are part of some assays in the paper, though ACO expression specifically is shown for salt stress and transgenic comparisons.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper clearly shows ET/ACC effects are dose-dependent and excessive ET/ACC reduces salt tolerance.

Primary_Substrate.reason: ACC oxidase identity supports ACC as substrate, but O2 is not explicitly stated in the paper text.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: ACO is discussed as part of ethylene biosynthesis, and higher ACO expression is linked to higher ethylene production.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results connect upregulated ACO genes in OE-miR319 and PvPCF5-SRDX plants with increased ethylene production and improved salt tolerance.

Primary_Product.reason: Ethylene is supported, but cyanide and CO2 are not stated in the paper.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: ACO is described as part of ethylene synthesis and, by enzyme name, catalyzes the terminal conversion of ACC to ethylene; the exact chemistry is standard and inferable, though not fully spelled out.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Results/Discussion state two tested ACO genes were induced early by salt stress, ACO transcripts were upregulated in OE-miR319 and 5sr plants, and this correlated with higher ethylene and better salt tolerance.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The text states three ACO1-encoding DEGs were up-regulated and that two ACO genes were tested by qRT-PCR; specific locus IDs are not provided in the excerpt.
